publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 20, 2024 at 02:01

did.unittitle: Pharmaceutical and chemical company publications

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes brochures, pamphlets, and promotional materials produced by pharmaceutical companies and chemical companies producing medicines and medical products.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was created by the Medical Historical Library. Provenance for individual items in the collection is unknown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in alphabetical order by company name.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes brochures, pamphlets, and promotional materials produced by pharmaceutical companies and chemical companies producing medicines and medical products.

did.unittitle: Abbott Laboratories, Chicago, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional materials for pentothal sodium, includes 1 brochure and 11 faux vacation postcards mailed to physicians.

did.unittitle: Arlington Chemical Company, Yonkers, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on plants which cause hayfever, includes section on Arlco-pollens "originated to make possible the scientific study of hayfever" with a product list and a list of proteins for cutaneous tests.

did.unittitle: Bilhuber-Knoll Company, Jersey City, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 brochure with anatomical plates of the circulation, nervous system, muscles, and skeleton advertising bromural, dilaudid, metrazol, octin, oenethyl, and theocalcin, 3 separate pages of anatomical plates of the circulatory system and nervous system.

did.unittitle: Boericke and Runyon, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet with catalogue and price lists of tinctures, tablets, medicine cases, and other materials for physicians

did.unittitle: Bristol Laboratories, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on the serum treatment of syphilis with sections on "the story of anti-syphilitic serum," "remarks on the claims for anti-syphilitic serum," and "instructions for using anti-syphilitic serum"

did.unittitle: Burnham Solube Iodine Company, Boston, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on iodine therapy and using Burnham's soluble iodine to treat high blood pressure, goiter, tertiary syphilis, chronic rheumatism and rheumatoid arthritis, chronic bronchitis, broncial asthma and certain infections

did.unittitle: Burroughs Wellcome and Company, Tukahoe, New York and Burlingame, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet with treatments for leukemia

did.unittitle: Chicona Products Institute, Incorporated, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on quinine and malaria and the examination of blood

did.unittitle: Ciba Pharmaceutical Products, Incorporated, Summit, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 brochures shaped like organs (the testicle, the uterus and fallopian tubes, the stomach, the heart, the kidney, the lungs, and the ovary) with illustrations of the outside of the organs and pharmaceutical products targeting them on the outside, and illustrations of the inside of the organs and descriptions of their anatomy on the inside flaps

did.unittitle: Ciba Pharmaceutical Products, Incorporated, Summit, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 brochures on the major pathology of the lungs, endocrine physiology in the female, small intestine, heart and aorta, nose and accessory sinuses, testicle and prostate with full color anatomical plates describing conditions and pharmaceutical treatments, 1 brochure on matendren linguets for treating liver disease, 1 pamphlet on epilepsy and elipten

did.unittitle: Colorado Serum Company Institute, Denver, Colorado

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet catalogue of products including animal blood, washed pooled blood cells, tissue culture products, bovine fetal serum, balanced salt solutions, material for fluorescent antibody technic, and whole serum anti-serum

did.unittitle: Commercial Solvents Corporation Pharmaceuticals, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on penicillin therapy and clinical applications in internal medicine, venereal and genitourinary diseases, otorhinolaryngology and ophthalmology, dermatology, surgery, and gynecology

did.unittitle: Dios Chemical Company, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 pamphlets on bromine, diagnosis and treatment of neurological conditions (including neurasthenia, hysteria, insomnia, epilepsy, menopause neuroses, chronic alcoholism), the history of bromide therapy, stories about difficulties facing young doctors starting their practice (including advice on marriage and dealing with women) with small notes on prescribing pharmaceutical treatments, syndromes and signs of nervous diseases, and guides to the diagnosis of nervous diseases

did.unittitle: Doho Chemical Corporation, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Foldout of nasal anatomy advertising rhinalgan

did.unittitle: Eaton Laboratories, Norwich, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pamphlets with vignetttes of medical histories of Benjamin Franklin, Napoleon Bonaparte, W.A. Mozart, General John J. Pershing, and Henry VIII

did.unittitle: Eli Lilly and Company, Indianopolis, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pamphlets advertising Eli Lilly products, including oxyl-iodide, iletin, liquid blaud, coco-quinine, and coco-vitamin, foldout announcing the rabies vaccine, diphtheria antitoxin, and smallpox vaccine

did.unittitle: F. Hoffman-La Roche Chemical Works, Basel, Switzerland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet in German on thiocol as an antidiarrhetic and treatment for tuberculosis and 1 pamphlet of "The Conquest of Pain," a one-act drama with the characters of Pain, Dentists, Patients, General Anesthesia, Ether, Chloroform, Nitrous Oxide, Local [Anesthesia], and Cocaine

did.unittitle: Frederick Stearns and Company, Detroit, Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet advertising neo-synephrine hydrochloride for nasal decongestion

did.unittitle: G.D. Searle and Company, Chicago, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 calendar with medical plates of pylorospasm, the inner ear, the small intestine, and the biliary system; 1 set of the same plates without the calendar; 1 brochure advertising ketochol to control drainage of the hepatobiliary tract; 1 pamphlet with excerpts from medical literature on using diodoquin to treat amebiasis; 2 pamphlets advertising alidase for hypodermoclysis; 1 pamphlet with illustrations of the digestive tract and ads for various medicines to treat issues with digestion, asthma, amebiasis, and bacterial infections

did.unittitle: G.W. Carnick Company, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 page of "single gland products" with order form on the back and 1 pamphlet with articles on and treatments for metabolism

did.unittitle: The Harrower Laboratory Incorporated, Glendale, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on hormones in relation to the ovaries, mucous colitis and dysovarism, hyperthyroidism, stomach issues, adrenal therapy for drug addicts, and premature sexual impotence

did.unittitle: Hectine Laboratories, Villeneuve-la-Garenne, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 page with excerpt from "The Action of Certain New Arsenical Compounds in the Therapeusis of Malaria," 1 pamphlet on using Hectine and Hectargyre to prevent and cure syphilis

did.unittitle: H.K. Mulford Company, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet of Mulford's Working Bulletin No. 2, 5th edition on Tuberculin and Tuberculin therapy, 1 page advertising how a team of dog sleds brought Mulford Super-Concentrated Diphtheria Antitoxin to Nome, which was snowed-in and dealing with a diphtheria epidemic

did.unittitle: Hudson Vitamin Products, Incorporated, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure on new vitamin products, including types of aspirin, dermatological ointments, and sweetners

did.unittitle: Lederle Laboratories, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on hetrazan and treating loa loa infections, onchocerca volvulus infections, ascariasis, and parasitic nematodes,1 bulletin on the reductionn in price of aureomycin, 1 pamphlet in Spanish on complicating factors of vitamin B,1 laminated page with table of dosages of aureomycin,1 bulletin on the toxemias of pregnancy with four medicines for diptheria, tetanus, whooping cough, powdered penicillin, and vitamin supplements

did.unittitle: Loeser Laboratory, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on parenteral sulphur therapy, including treatments for arthritis, lesions of the central nervous system, psychosis, and peripheral vascular disease

did.unittitle: Mead Johnson and Company, Evansville, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four postcards with fish on the front advertising fish liver oil to treat rickets, tetany, and osteomalacia

did.unittitle: Merck and Company, Incorporated, Rahway, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on the treatment of war injuries, including traumatic shock, war wounds, burns, and poison gas casualties, 1 foldout brochure on erythrol tetranitrate with illustrations of the nervous system, heart and tissues with blood vessels, 1 pamphlet with a progress report on cortisone, 1 pamphlet advertising Merck's methods

did.unittitle: The Newskin Company, Brooklyn, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on New-Skin, a liquid which forms a waterproof surface, and its applications for surgery, general medicine, and laboratories

did.unittitle: Parke, Davis and Company, Detroit, Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 brochure advertising mapharsen as a spirocheticidal treatment for syphilis

did.unittitle: Petrolagar Laboratories, Incorporated, Chicago, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pamphlets with color illustrations of the anatomy and conditions affecting the large bowel, digestive tract, and lower bowel, rectum, and anal canal.

did.unittitle: Pfizer Laboratories, Brooklyn, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pamphlets with with anatomical and pathological transparencies, one for chronic pulmonary tuberculosis and one for the head and neck, 1 pamphlet advertising asthma treatments Marax and Atarax using histories of medicine in Babylon and Pergamum, including replicas of coins from those regions

did.unittitle: Reed and Carnrick

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet with anatomical plates advertising trophonine and protonuclein

did.unittitle: The Robin Laboratories, Bourges

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet advertising bismuthoidol to treat syphilis

did.unittitle: S.B. Penick and Company, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet on neomycin, an antibiotic, its chemical and pharmacological propoerties, and clinical and veterinary applications

did.unittitle: Schering Corporation, Bloomfield, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 pamplets with illustrations of normal and abnormal conditions for pelvic anatomy, the genitourinary tract, arthritis, cardiopulmonary disorders, female pelvic and obstetrical anatomy and male genitalia, rheumatism, and EENT (eye, ear, nose, and throat) disorders, 1 page advertising sulfadiazine as a treatment for pneumonia

did.unittitle: Sharp and Dohme, Baltimore, Maryland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet advertising hexylresorcinol solution to treat burns and scalds, 1 pamphet on various products to treat whooping cough, allergies, Parkinson's disease and more, 1 pamphlet with products to treat infectious diseases, including diphtheria, scarlet fever, smallpox, typhoid fever, tetanus, whooping cough, the common cold, and influenza

did.unittitle: Smith, Kline, and French Laboratories, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet advertising compazine as an antiemetic and a tranquilizer to treat anxiety and mood disorders, 1 page advertising compazine as both a long-term and short-term therapy

did.unittitle: Winthrop Laboratories, New York, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 brochure advertising prontosil and prontylin as treatments for gonorrhea, 1 pamphlet advertising avertin as a basal anesthetic for rectal administration, 1 pamphlet with instructions for treatment of patients who have overdosed on their products, 1 pamphlet of an annotated bibliography of penicillin literature

did.unittitle: W.M. S. Merrell Company, Cincinnati, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pamphlet with humourous combinations of pictures and text on doctors and patients with an advertisement for bentyl at the end, which was supposed to treat smooth muscle spasms
